title: Off topic Manga AU discussion Shinji Ikari Raising Project
display_title: Off topic Manga AU discussion Shinji Ikari Raising Project
wikitext:
[]: Off-topic Manga AU discussion: Shinji Ikari Raising Project             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Sep 19, 2012 12:44 pm Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: EoTV AU is Shinji Ikari Raising Project. Or at least getting the feel and stuff of it right; IMO at least. My problem with Angelic Days is probably partly the same as Rj's. It felt like it couldnt make up its mind on what t wanted to be. Also the OTP dynamic/relationship between Shinji and Asuka felt forced/rushed. I might nag about SIRP's fanservice and such at times, but i like the slow pace. And it is what EoTV AU is what I felt it was meant to be; a harem romantic comedy. Though I do feel like it could use a bit more direction. Seeings as they have SEELE interloping sparingly and was the whole purpose of Kaworus existence apparently. But there is more of a build up and natural feel to the A/S/R dynamic. And hopefully if he ever does choose it will be handled better then Angelic Days was.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 2:12 am Because it's fucking stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:41 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Because it's fucking stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:48 pm Rj123541 wrote: Well I am sure it is because it looks a little like angelic days but I don't think it can be worse than angelic days.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:52 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: It's not like Angelic Days/Iron Maiden 2nd, actually I don't know if gatotsu911 was referring to Iron Maiden 2nd or to the title of the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:53 pm If you like fanservice and moe then you should be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Fri Sep 21, 2012 12:57 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: If you like fanservice and moe then you should be fine.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Sep 22, 2012 2:51 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I don't know if gatotsu911 was referring to Iron Maiden 2nd or to the title of the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Sep 22, 2012 3:08 pm gatotsu911 wrote: The latter. Unfortunately I couldn't clarify and keep my response suitably punchy at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 22, 2012 5:25 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: This is not good, the manga of ISIK is not bad at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Sep 22, 2012 5:36 pm Bagheera wrote: It's utter trash if you have no interest in fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Sep 22, 2012 5:42 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Something is definitely wrong with a man if he doesn't have any interest in fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Sun Sep 23, 2012 12:37 am After all the feedback I have got on it I am thinking of passing on it but I should probably finish it before I can make my final judgment on it.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sun Sep 23, 2012 9:04 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Something is definitely wrong with a man if he doesn't have any interest in fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Sep 23, 2012 9:23 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: Well the doors open on this one so... What if it has only Asuka fanservice? does that change things.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Sep 25, 2012 7:07 am Rj123541 wrote: I just hope if i read it I don't feel this way.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Thu Sep 27, 2012 3:09 pm Warren Peace wrote: I avoid this "expanded universe" stuff entirely because I'm afraid it'll make me feel that way. I mean, I've accumulated a lot of Eva mental junk over the years from trolls and weird theories and inappropriate fan art just sticking to the main series. I feel like reading this stuff will just lead to more weird tangents. I'm not criticizing anyone who gets something out of it, only speaking for myself. It's just that I've barely come to terms with the real Eva without piling stuff on top of it! If the offshoots formed a new way of looking at the original show, that might be worth it. But people don't really characterize it that way, it's more "oh, isn't this novel?"

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Sep 27, 2012 8:16 pm Rj123541 wrote: While we are at it have you read Neon Genesis Evangelion: Campus Apocalypse ? It looks pretty bad to me as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Tue Oct 02, 2012 1:22 pm EvangelionFan wrote: The first two volumes are decent but it is something you'll probably only read once.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Oct 02, 2012 9:27 pm Rj123541 wrote: It looks to me like something I probably wouldn't read at all. But I will have to give it a try, even if I don't like the fact that Anno sold his soul to the audience.

Also the OTP dynamic/relationship between Shinji and Asuka felt forced/rushed. I might nag about SIRP's fanservice and such at times, but i like the slow pace. And it is what EoTV AU is what I felt it was meant to be; a harem romantic comedy. Though I do feel like it could use a bit more direction. Seeings as they have SEELE interloping sparingly and was the whole purpose of Kaworus existence apparently. But there is more of a build up and natural feel to the A/S/R dynamic. And hopefully if he ever does choose it will be handled better then Angelic Days was.
